_note: Outdoor feed: ski Dec-Apr, backpack May-Oct. Western US focus. Refreshed every ~3 weeks by GHA cron.

items.headline: 2026 Hiking Gear Trends: Alpha Direct, Mini Pumps, and GPS Watches That Actually Matter
items.snark: Alpha Direct sweaters are absurdly light and warm but tear like tissue paper. Mini air pumps for sleeping pads changed the game. Garmin watches finally justify the price for long hauls.

items.headline: Best Backpacking Packs for Thru-Hiking in 2026: Hyperlite Southwest Crushes the Survey
items.snark: The Hyperlite Mountain Gear Southwest got the highest satisfaction rating from AT hikers this year. Turns out bombproof simplicity still wins over feature bloat.

items.headline: Continental Divide Trail Days 2026 Recap: Hiker Olympics Feature Cathole Digging Contest
items.snark: Silver City just hosted cathole digging competitions and pie eating contests. The CDT community celebrates the absurdities of trail life with judges measuring hole depth and awarding technique points.

items.headline: CDT Class of 2026 Adjusts Start Dates: Record Low Snow in San Juans Moves Timeline Up
items.snark: Snowpack in the San Juans is so pathetically low that NOBOs are starting two weeks early. Colorado high country in mid-April used to be suicide, now it's just another Tuesday.

items.headline: PCT Class of 2026 Kicks Off: Light Snow Year in Sierra, Heavy Flood Damage in SoCal
items.snark: Light snow year means easier Kennedy Meadows arrivals, but recent flooding left Southern California trail surfaces scoured and loose. Desert hikers are submitting water reports like their lives depend on it, because they do.

items.headline: The Best Backpacking Tents for 2026: Durston X Mid and Zpacks Pivot Dominate
items.snark: Years of AT thru-hiker surveys confirm what the commenters already knew. Durston X Mid trekking pole setup stays solid in wind, Zpacks DCF is light enough to carry externally and tough enough to survive the whole trail.

items.headline: Backpacking the Pecos: A Wilderness of Enchantment with Fewer Crowds Than Colorado
items.snark: Over 200,000 acres of tundra ridges, 13ers, and bighorn sheep an hour from Santa Fe. Lower elevations accessible mid-May, high country from Memorial Day to early October. No glossy travelogues feature it, which is exactly the point.

items.headline: San Juan Mountains Opening Late May: Roads Clear, Wildflowers Brief, Temps Still Cold
items.snark: Secondary forest roads opening late May or June as usual. Summer and fall are fleeting above treeline. Bring extra layers because high elevation doesn't care what the town thermometer says.

items.headline: Continental Divide Trail Advice from Class of 2025: Colorado Will Kick Your Butt
items.snark: Don't rush to Chama unless you want a difficult snow experience. Take it easy in New Mexico while the high country melts. Colorado is hard no matter how strong you feel, and you'll sleep with your water filter every night including summer.

items.headline: Weminuche Trail Provides Jumping-Off Point for Granite Lake, Divide Lakes Destinations
items.snark: Trail 592 drops elevation for the first two miles before entering the Weminuche Wilderness proper. Keep that in mind for the hike out after a long day crushing miles above treeline.

items.headline: Hiking, Backpacking, and Exploring Enchanted New Mexico: Bandelier Backcountry Uncrowded
items.snark: Recent fires and floods mean Bandelier's backcountry isn't crowded. The only way to see major highlights like the Painted Cave is backpacking deep into the canyons. Twenty-mile round trip but worth every step through red rock and cottonwoods.
